transcript.text:
[Dear all small business owners, cheer up!]
[Tzuyang is with Small & Medium Business Distribution Center and WeMap (WeMakePrice)]
[To serve the public interest and improve the economy via "Value Based Consumption Campaign"]
This video serves as a content of public service advertisement and has been made in collaboration with Small & Medium Business Distribution Center (SBDC) and WeMakePrice (WeMap) to promote "Value Based Consumption Campaign"
Hello everyone~
Today, I am here to promote a public advertisement called "Value Based Consumption Campaign"
which was made in collaboration with SBDC and WeMap to encourage all small business owners
Ansagomohani (What are you hesitating over?) Season 2 is back!
So I bought $1,500 worth of foods, cosmetics, etc.
Because small business owners have been struggling a lot since the outbreak of COVID-19,
I am very pleased and thankful to participate in this campaign with such a great opportunity
Also, there's a promotion event that you can participate in
Make a hand heart gesture like this
It's hard to do it the other way
Take a picture of it with any products inside the heart or just bare hands
and post the picture on Instagram with appropriate hashtags

They will randomly draw 100 participants and present Apple Watch SE
I'll probably participate too haha
I can't eat all this today but I will take a little bit of each product and describe its taste
It's a lot of food
I don't even know what to start with
Let me clean up the table real quick tho
I'll start with these two first
These are bonless chicken feet and spicy stir-fried pork intestine from Pugeon Food Co.
Warm it in the microwave for 3 minutes
It's been so long since I had pork intestine
I'm so excited
5 minutes should be enough for both
I also got some salmon sashimi made by Wolf Corporation
I can eat it straight from the plate
It comes with capers and horseradish sauce
This beverage is called Voilyn oolong tea (made from leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant) made by Korea Food Tech. Inc.

I've never had oolong tea for mukbang
Wow, the color is very dark. What is this?
It looks more like juice
What a deep, savory taste
It carries a strong taste/scent
Let's try the food
It looks super chewy
No way
It's yummy since I haven't had it for a while I love it
The sauce must be good too
Although I don't know the standard portion size for 1 person,
it's pretty good amount to eat when drinking alone
It's very time-efficient because it only takes 3 minutes to make a decent drinking snack
This can't go wrong
Yummy
Salmon

Look, it's so thick
The portion is pretty big
Dip it in the sauce
It's very soft and tender
It also has some type of unique smoky flavor too
I love it
Look, it's so big. It fills my entire mouth
Tasty
I was worried about the strong smell of fish once I opened it, but there's no need to worry
It's really good
Let's try the gopchang this time. Look at the "gop" inside
This is way better than what I expected.
man, this makes me feel like drinking
This is a bit spicy tho. A piece of salmon should help me with the spiciness
Dang, it's still spicy
I finished it so fast because it's so tasty

A piece of gopchang
For real, I love it so much that it's better than delivery
Salmon always makes me happy
Raw duck made by Opumduck and Chuncheon dakgalbi (spicy stir-fried chicken) made by Modoofnb Inc.
And lastly, Korean short rib patties made by sulchangchuksan
Since the spicy stir-fried chicken takes a while, I'll cook it first
It is crucial not to burn it!
Sweet pumpkin sauce!
You can add rice cakes, cheese or whatever you want. I think I saw some rice cakes somewhere here
I found a bag of rice cakes
It's called Nangmanbubu mozzarella cheese sliced rice cake recipe
made by Pot Where Buckwheat Flower Blooms
Dang, the cheese is sticking out
I'll put it on the plate since it's almost done
I'll roast this together next time
There's no pork tho

It took a while to cook it
Bon appetit
I gotta try this like right now. It's sweet pumpkin cream cheese dakgalbi
It's not bad at all
The pumpkin sauce is not strong, but rather delicate and subtle, and that goes very well with dakgalbi.
Look at the cheese inside of it
The cheese is as thick as my finger
The rice cake is very chewy and delicious
Let's try the duck meat
Though I love all kinds of meat,
I also don't like the ones that are too tough
But this one is really tasty
Like for real
Why is everything so tasty today?
The next one is tteokgalbi
I can tell that it's super juicy by squeezing the meat

Yummy
Literally everything is super tasty. Every single one of them is so juicy
and it's very tender and soft
Dip it in the sauce fully
Next
This gejang (Soy Sauce-Marinated Crabs) is made by Third Generation Incheon Gejang
It's pretty big
Jokbal (pork feet)!
It's called Dure boneless jokbal made by Dure Food Corporation
I forgot to hit the record button and ate all of it
so I only have the empty shells left
They were full of roe and I loved it
I'll actually eat 1 more marinated gejang
Yangnyeom-gejang (Spicy Sauce-Marinated Crabs)
It's full of meat
It's better than the soy sauce one

Once again, it's full of meat and a bit spicy
Last but not least, jokbal (pork feet)
Wow, it's literally all meat
Literally boneless!
The whole thing is pork feet skin
Am I supposed to cut it first to eat?
It's dessert time
The bottled peaches must be really tasty
and Nyammongmong,
Yeot, and some chocolate
Oh, I've tried this macaron before. I really loved it because its coque was super chewy.
Look at the color! Isn't it pretty
This is called Narin Ginseng Chocolate made by Narin Inc.
I see a bunch of small chocolate balls
2 packs!
This one is called mokpyeonyeot (Yeot that makes your throat comfortable)

Oh wow, it looks like a caramel not like an actual yeot
Even the package is similar to that of a typical caramel
So it does taste like yeot, but the texture is somewhere in between yeot and caramel
This is the ginseng chocolate
I don't know if I'm gonna like it tho because I don't like ginseng
At first, the ginseng scent burst in my mouth
but, at the end, it tasted like an actual chocolate. Very interesting.
It's like a "No pain, No gain" chocolate
Let me try the macaron
The one that I tried last time from this company was the thinner one but this one is very thick
Tasty
I love this company's macaron because it's not too sweet
It's very good
I can't believe that it's canned food because it looks very fresh
Amazing
Classic bottled peaches

It's completely different from Hwangdo (Canned yellow peaches)
Alright, I will stop here and call it a day
I still have a lot of different products in the back that I didn't get to try and introduce
and there's a lot more stuff on the website
As you know, small business owners have been struggling a lot since the outbreak of COVID-19.
So please check out the website and try them out. It will be definitely worth a try
Since I can't use or eat all the stuffs in the back,
I'll go to my restaurant to give them away to customers and promote the products
Thank you everyone!
I arrived at my restaurant
Let me hurry up and give them out
I'm trying to do a random draw to give away products made by small business owners
12 and 17 for you?
As I said, these are made by small business owners who are having a hard time
Please use the product and if you like it, talk about it with people around you please
Thank you

Hello~ Hi!
It's very heavy
Is that okay? Absolutely
And this is coffee
This is a humidifier
A humidifier? Yep
This is already good, no doubt
And this is a wish bracelet. Really?
Thank you so much
Thank you
Thank you !
Please use it and let your friends know about it if you like it!
For sure, thank you
I think it means a lot.
This is such a great opportunity to know different types of small business and their products
I will definitely let my friends know if I like it!

We should often utilize website such as goods, where lots of creators, artists, and small businesses are selling their products
It must be nice to go attend their fan meetings once the pandemic situation gets mitigated
Thanks for this
That was the last one! Once again, take a look at the website
Dear all small business owners, hang in there! Let's turn the crisis into an opportunity with WeMap
